$comment: Static policy encoding which host→client payload paths are catalog-gated (id-agnostic — future harnesses never touch this file). Not a per-change review mechanism. Method/path support globs (* one segment, ** any depth; method * is non-dot). Catalog methods agent.gui.listHarnesses / agent.list / providers.list must never appear here for host→client payloads — growth on those requires freezing the shipped line and opening a new major with downgrade bridges (amp 003d7586 / devin 407d110 template).

exceptions.reason: Plan source harnessId is gated by which harnesses the host actually lists; old clients only open getPlan for harnesses they already know.
Commands are scoped to a harness the client already selected from a negotiated catalog; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Models are scoped to a harness the client already selected from a negotiated catalog; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Models are scoped to a harness the client already selected; additive harness ids on this echo field stay behind that gate.
Rate-limit rows are only returned for providers the client has already configured; additive provider ids stay behind that gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Provider mutation responses echo a single providerId the client just named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
awaitLogin echoes the single providerId the client named in the request; additive enum values on the echo stay behind the request gate.
Chat stream frames only carry harness ids for chats the client already opened against a negotiated harness catalog; additive ids stay behind that gate.
Action-ack kinds grow with gated client actions; old clients only observe acks for actions they can issue.
Trigger kinds grow with gated product surfaces; old clients only observe kinds their client version can raise.
Runtime event union grows with gated harness/event features; old clients only subscribe to chats whose harnesses they can open.
Per-harness message anchor variants grow with gated harnesses; old clients only open those harnesses from a negotiated catalog.
Per-harness session-anchor variants grow with gated harnesses; old clients only open those harnesses from a negotiated catalog.
Content-block union variants grow with gated product surfaces; old clients only render blocks their version knows for chats they can open.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream: the chat stream client parses every serverFrame through the live schema, and the field is nullable/.default(null) where null IS the pre-profile semantic (ambient/legacy session) - an old 1.3 host's frames materialize exactly what a pre-profile session genuinely has. Old 1.3 clients parse with plain (stripping) objects, so a new host's frames degrade cleanly. Streams have no per-frame version transforms, so a minor bump would change nothing at runtime; the exception is scoped by field path, so unrelated additions stay guarded.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, frames are live-schema-parsed both directions; scoped by field path.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, frames are live-schema-parsed both directions; scoped by field path.

Persistence-mirror profile field on a parsed stream (see the **.profileId entry for the full rationale): nullable/.default(null), null is the pre-profile semantic, the GUI additionally falls back to its own palette hash when null; scoped by field path.
